title: Episode 19 Tojis dream
wikitext:
[]: Episode 19 / Toji's "dream"             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Wed Jun 18, 2014 11:59 pm So near the beginning of Episode 19, Toji lies in the hospital bed. He sees Shinji in the bed next to his. Toji passes out and dreams that he is on the Hell Train. He sees Rei and Shinji talking on the train, and they discuss what Shinji has done, how he feels about his father, what his father has done, etc. As far as I can tell, there's no way that Toji could have had foreknowledge of things being discussed on that train (I doubt that Toji even knows about how Shinji feels about his father's praise), and its odd that the Hell Train would even manifest in his dream. This leaves me with 2 questions: 1. Why is Toji hearing about these things in his dream? Is there some kind of acting force or connection that I am unaware of that is telling him this or is he just coincidentally dreaming up a scenario that happens to fit right in with Shinji's turmoil? 2. Why are we shown this as Toji's dream and not Shinji's? The sequence seems like it would make perfect sense as Shinji's dream instead. Why did Anno chose to have this scene in Toji's mind?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jun 19, 2014 4:56 am Maybe LCL connects the thoughts of the pilots somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:09 am I've always interpreted that scene as being from Touji's perspective. Yeah, it looks like it's in "the crazy room", but it's actually taking place, in reality, Rei is visiting Shinji in the hospital, but Touji is kind of drugged up, so the environment looks different.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 19, 2014 8:56 am That was always one of the more mysterious scenes that really served a more atmospheric/abstract purpose, the point being in how it looks and what the two are exchanging, but some ppl have gone an extrapolated the wildest things from this alone, like 'Rei is telepathic', or 'those are actually Shinji & Rei from instrumentality, reliving the rest of the story in flashback or flash-forward' If you believe shared angel headspace, it could be something similar, only with EVAs, although the most honest conclusion might be that we don't really know. Usually, the train seems to be strictly Shinji's headspace/"mental world", given that it seems based off the one Gendo put him in as a kid or the one he rode around in in episode 4. What we see here could be everything from an aesthetic choice, or a vital clue to the place's true nature. Personally, it might've something to do with Bardiel seeping it's slimy corruption into EVA 01 while it was strangling it, or at least the fact that both Shinji and Touji had just been removed from their EVAs. EVAs commonly trigger visions, regardless of what those visions are. In any case, the scene's purpose in the narrative is to establish/vocally present some of Shinji's turmoil/thoughts about the Bardiel fiasco to us, and I really don't want to take away from that by turning it into some 'magic' event on vague premises.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 19, 2014 1:02 pm Related thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2123/Why-Could-Toji-See-Shinjis-Dream/

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Thu Jun 19, 2014 6:07 pm Also related thread - http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15800/Is-Rei-Lilith-dead-after-EoE/? Mostly because it raises the other theory some of use were playing with linking it to the Bookend Reis - AuraTwilight wrote: Also wasn't there a part during Toji's hospitalization that he saw a vision of Shinji talking to Rei in a train scene....and current Rei very clearly had no idea any of that was happening? Like if nothing else that's pretty much solid evidence of a Rei-Entity that isn't Current Rei is doing shenanigans.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Jun 21, 2014 11:42 pm I didn't get a chance to look through the other threads, but when I first saw the scene in question, I figured that it had to do with some kind of residual subconscious memories/insight caused by Bardiel's possession of Unit-03/Touji. IIRC during the fight against Unit-03 (re-classified as the 13th Angel) Bardiel briefly invaded/attempted to infect both Unit-00 (before its arm was severed) and then Unit-01 (before the dummy plug was activated). Given how the fifteenth and sixteenth angels were later shown to have made contact with the minds of Asuka and Rei respectively (and I suppose you could even argue that the twelfth/Sea of Dirac angel was trying to accomplish the same thing with Shinji), I guess I assumed that Bardiel had for just a moment managed to link the minds of Shinji and Rei with that of Touji, and his "train of thought" dream sequence while in NERV's sickbay was a reflection of that temporary connection (even if he didn't really remember or understand what was happening).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 23, 2014 8:24 am Kendrix wrote: That was always one of the more mysterious scenes that really served a more atmospheric/abstract purpose, the point being in how it looks and what the two are exchanging, but some ppl have gone an extrapolated the wildest things from this alone, like 'Rei is telepathic'

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 23, 2014 9:16 am In the next scene, were are directly shown Shinji and Touji's proximity in their hospital room. The pieces are being shown in place Ep 19: Touji's location  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jun 23, 2014 9:39 am My interpretation of the above sequences is that Toji has inexperienced a kind of Psychic overbroadcast from Rei as she converses with a sleeping Shinji, inside Shinji's own mind. The key piece of evidence is Toji's being inside the Hell Train, something he needs access to Shinji's mind in order to see. We don't have evidence that Toji's time in Bardiel has given him such abilities or insight, but we do have a scene immediately prior with raises the mysterious nature of Rei's dreams, just before we see her in a dream which Toji could not possibly have by himself. Both of the Angels Leilel and Arael communicate with humans in a similar manner, and Armisael is also shown doing so with Rei. To a lesser extent, even Eva-01 communicates with Rei in a similar fashion in #14. As an angel, Rei's ability to communicate like this has precedent, and neatly explains why Toji's sees what he sees here. Finally, it's worth noting that Rei's ability to communicate with a dreaming Shinji appears again in NTE Rebuild. In 1.11, Rei appears and converses with Shinji in the Helltrain, and is revealed to be by his bedside when he wakes. [wkimg:15fqxwoy]File:R1-11 C1324.jpg[/wkimg:15fqxwoy] [wkimg:15fqxwoy]File:R1-11 C1342 b.jpg[/wkimg:15fqxwoy] And in 2.22, Rei again appears in Shinji's dream, where he (mentally) abandons the SDAT Player. Later, Rei is seen searching for and finding the SDAT which Shinji did end up (physically) throwing away. [wkimg:15fqxwoy]File:R2-22 C1308G.jpg[/wkimg:15fqxwoy] [wkimg:15fqxwoy]File:R2-22 C1363.jpg[/wkimg:15fqxwoy] [wkimg:15fqxwoy]File:R2-22 C1364.jpg[/wkimg:15fqxwoy] Admittedly, these are instances from Rebuild and not NGE proper, but they do illustrate the basic idea of Rei's ability to communicate with Shinji inside the Hell Train. In NGE itself, outside of Instrumentality, this is actually only ever shown in Toji's hallucination. That's why its interpretation is so critical. If it really is something "psychic" or "synch-ic", then it's a huge hint towards the nature and abilites of Rei in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jun 23, 2014 9:54 am ^ Conclusion: Rei is such a creepy stalker! More seriously, awesome job OMF, about the Rebuild, Rei having psychic powers could explain the mysterious disembodied voice Shinji hears in 3.0 just before calling for Rei (triggering Mark.09's appearance)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 23, 2014 10:52 am Interesting thoughts but why not Hell Train/Quantum Rei being Rei 3-Lilith as the common line of thinking goes? About Eva NTE: Don't forget in 1.11 we hear/see Rei after Yui's words in the first Hell Train dream ("Ayanami Shinji, Ikari Rei", creepy looking Rei, etc).

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 24, 2014 1:06 am Stealing this for my fic. Fantastic job, OMF. Now all of a sudden I'm back into all this Discussion shit. You've made it interesting again. And just today I was wondering if I'd ever find that spark again, after everything I've been through lately changing my life priorities. One question, though: Why did you even mention that bit of (alleged) "misdirection"? Even if Asuka really was talking about Shinji when she said "that idiot won't recover" (and I think she was, if only because any misdirection here seems completely pointless), I don't see how it would affect the rest of the analysis or what it has to do with the topic at all.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jun 24, 2014 2:06 am I agree it's most likely Toji is just somehow caught in the effect, but I think it's more plausible that the Rei in the train is UbeRei, the post-instrumentality ascended being who exists outside the bounds of space and time, and whom is just taking the opportunity to check in and try and understand Shinji better... the very thing she wonders at his inability to do with Gendo. Note that Shinji doesn't show any sign that he remembers any of this, so it's possible she's been doing these visits now and then for the entire length of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 24, 2014 3:10 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: About Eva NTE: Don't forget in 1.11 we hear/see Rei after Yui's words in the first Hell Train dream ("Ayanami Shinji, Ikari Rei", creepy looking Rei, etc).

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:24 pm ElMariachi wrote: More seriously, awesome job OMF, about the Rebuild, Rei having psychic powers could explain the mysterious disembodied voice Shinji hears in 3.0 just before calling for Rei (triggering Mark.09's appearance)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jul 09, 2014 9:35 pm NemZ and Stardust beat me to it. You're not thinking fifth dimensionally!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jul 09, 2014 10:26 pm I'll agree that Rebuild Rei seems to have these psychic powers, but I don't believe that holds true for pre-apotheiosis Rei in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 10, 2014 12:38 am Chuckman wrote: You're not thinking fifth dimensionally!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jul 10, 2014 12:46 am For Rebuild, we can't discount the possibility that Rei will become fifth dimensional without an nth impact and mass harvesting of souls. I really doubt Anno's entire plan for her was to effectively die off screen between movies.
